core_mechanism: structural identifiability analysis of a parametrized dynamical system through its input-output (observation) map, followed by a reparameterization onto identifiable parameter combinations that converts a non-invertible parameter-to-output map into a globally invertible one, so the inverse estimation problem has a unique, well-conditioned solution
brief_reason: The abstract performs a structural identifiability analysis showing the parameters are not globally identifiable and derives a globally identifiable reparameterization via an observational-system approach that eliminates the non-uniqueness of the parameter-to-data inversion.
